title: Skyrocketing component prices push Big Tech capex to record $725 billion — Microsoft alone attributes $25 billion of AI budget to increased memory and chip costs

title: US Economy 'Incredibly Resilient': Mossavar-Rahmani
snippet: Sharmin Mossavar-Rahmani, wealth management CIO and head of the investment strategy group at Goldman Sachs, says the US economy has been "incredibly resilient" despite the impact of the Iran war. She joined "Bloomberg S…
contentText: Sharmin Mossavar-Rahmani, wealth management CIO and head of the investment strategy group at Goldman Sachs, says the US economy has been "incredibly resilient" despite the impact of the Iran war. She joined "Bloomberg Surveillance" on April 30 to discuss how significant AI spending by Big Tech and restrictive monetary policies are shaping market dynamics. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: CIOs ready for another role-change as AI becomes agent of chaos

contentText: If software writes software the risk is “systematic failure at scale”. Someone needs to take charge, argues Forrester Forrester predicts that by decade's end, the rush toward agentic AI will grow so chaotic that CIOs will be forced into a new role as enforcer of order.…

title: What’s Tech’s Next iPhone Moment?
snippet: Will OpenAI ever bring a smartphone, or some other smartphone-like device, to market? On this episode of the Everybody's Business podcast, we are joined by Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman, the author of the Power On newsletter…
contentText: Will OpenAI ever bring a smartphone, or some other smartphone-like device, to market? On this episode of the Everybody's Business podcast, we are joined by Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman, the author of the Power On newsletter and managing editor for consumer technology. He tells Stacey Vanek Smith and Max Chafkin whether such a device could succeed and what the next iPhone moment might look like. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: OpenAI CFO Cites ‘Vertical Wall of Demand’ for Products

contentText: OpenAI Chief Financial Officer Sarah Friar sees “a vertical wall of demand” for the company’s products as she pushed back Thursday on concerns about missing internal targets in a Bloomberg interview. Ed Ludlow reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Caterpillar’s Biggest Bear Folds as AI Power Demand Soars
snippet: Caterpillar Inc.’s biggest Wall Street bear has thrown in the towel after watching the stock more than double on the back of surging demand for the company’s power generation equipment.

title: Thiel’s Founders Fund Raises $6 Billion in Its Largest-Ever Haul

contentText: Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund has raised $6 billion for a new fund to invest in later-stage companies, according to people familiar with the matter, marking the firm’s largest haul ever.

title: Tether’s Gold Hoard Nears $20 Billion as Buying Continues

contentText: Tether Holdings SA bought more than six tons of gold for its reserves in the first three months of the year, extending a buying streak that’s made it the largest known holder of bullion in the world outside of banks and nation states.

title: Dan Ives Sees Apple ‘Golden Age’ as It Monetizes Consumer AI
snippet: “The consumer AI revolution is gonna go through Cupertino,” says Dan Ives, global head of tech research and senior equity analyst at Wedbush Securities, as he discusses expectations for Apple’s AI strategy after the com…
contentText: “The consumer AI revolution is gonna go through Cupertino,” says Dan Ives, global head of tech research and senior equity analyst at Wedbush Securities, as he discusses expectations for Apple’s AI strategy after the company delivered a surprisingly strong revenue forecast for the third quarter. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia's H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo — Beijing pushes homegrown AI hardware dominance in a market projected to hit $67 billion by 2030

title: US Stocks Extend Record Gains, Oil Falls on Iran Talks Report
snippet: The S&P 500 Index extended record-breaking gains Friday as tech megacaps delivered a stream of solid earnings. Reports that Iran had responded to US amendments on an agreement to end the war also buoyed sentiment, sendi…
contentText: The S&P 500 Index extended record-breaking gains Friday as tech megacaps delivered a stream of solid earnings. Reports that Iran had responded to US amendments on an agreement to end the war also buoyed sentiment, sending oil prices lower.
